mnemonics.title: Three Schema Levels
mnemonics.content: E.C.I - Everyone Can Imagine (External, Conceptual, Internal) - Easy way to remember the order from user view to physical storage.

mnemonics.title: Data Independence Types
mnemonics.content: P.L.E - Physical changes don't affect Logical which doesn't affect External views.

mnemonics.title: Schema Order by Abstraction
mnemonics.content: U.C.P - User view to Conceptual to Physical - moving from high-level user perspective to low-level storage details.

acronyms.usage: The organization that proposed the three-schema architecture through SPARC in 1975.
The ANSI committee responsible for proposing the three-schema architecture.
Software system that implements the three-schema architecture to manage data.
Used to define schemas at conceptual and internal levels.
Used to manipulate data based on external schema views.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a three-layer cake: Top layer (External) - what users see, Middle layer (Conceptual) - the recipe/blueprint, Bottom layer (Internal) - actual ingredients and cooking method.
Think of a building: Physical = foundation/structure changes, Logical = floor plan changes, External = room decoration changes. Changes at one level don't affect others above.
Use a pyramid diagram: External at top (many views), Conceptual in middle (one global view), Internal at bottom (physical storage). Arrows show transformations between levels.

analogies.analogy: Like a company: External = different departments seeing only their work, Conceptual = organizational chart visible to all, Internal = actual office infrastructure and storage rooms.

Like building a house: Changing paint color (external) doesn't require changing walls (conceptual) or foundation (internal). Changing foundation affects walls but can be designed to preserve external views.
Like different apps on your phone - each shows different interface but same data exists behind them.
Like a blueprint of a building - shows the complete structure but not the actual construction materials or methods.
